description: Intact Auto insurance JSON output format configuration

last_name.extraction_logic: Extract the applicant's last name (surname). Priority: first check application form, if not found check other documents.

first_name.extraction_logic: Extract the applicant's first name. Priority: first check application form, if not found check other documents.

gender.extraction_logic: Extract the applicant's gender. Priority: first check application form, if not found check other documents. Must match one of the options: Female, Male, or X.

date_of_birth.extraction_logic: Extract the applicant's date of birth. Priority: first check application form, if not found check other documents. Convert to YYYY-MM-DD format.
date_of_birth.description: Applicant's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format

marital_status.extraction_logic: Extract the applicant's marital status. Priority: first check application form, if not found check other documents. Must match one of the options: Divorced, Married, Single, or Widow.

postal_code.description: Postal code in Canadian format X9X9X9

full_address.description: Full address information in format like '164 Hawthorn Ave, Stouffville, ON'

broker_number.extraction_logic: Extract the broker number from the application form. The broker code may start with a capital letter (e.g., 'X40460', 'A12345'). If it starts with a capital letter, ignore that letter and extract only the numeric part. Output should be 5 digits (e.g., 'X40460' should become '40460').
broker_number.description: 5-digit broker number (numeric part only, ignore leading capital letter if present)

edi_client_code.extraction_logic: Extract the EDI client code from the application form. Look for 'Code:' label followed by the code value (e.g., 'Code: ALEE01' should extract 'ALEE01').

policy_effective_date.description: Policy effective date in YYYY-MM-DD format

risk.description: Risk information - array of vehicles. Extract ALL vehicles found in documents. Each element represents one vehicle.
risk.extraction_logic: Extract ALL vehicles (risks) found in the documents. Each vehicle should be a separate element in the array. If only one vehicle is found, the array should contain one element. Each risk element should contain all the risk fields for that specific vehicle.

risk_type.extraction_logic: Extract the risk type from documents for THIS specific vehicle. If there is no clear evidence indicating a different vehicle type (ATV, ANT, CLA, MCY, MHO, SNO, PPT), default to 'PPV' (Private Passenger Vehicle).

serial_number.extraction_logic: Extract the vehicle VIN (Vehicle Identification Number) for THIS specific vehicle from the quote document. VIN is typically 17 characters long.

model.extraction_logic: Extract the full vehicle model descriptor for THIS specific vehicle. Include as much detail as available, ideally in a format like '2022 Toyota Corolla SE Hatchback 5dr FWD 2.0L I4'. If year/make/model/trim/body/drivetrain are available, combine them into one concise string.
model.description: Detailed vehicle model descriptor (year + make + model + trim/body/drivetrain when available)

condition_when_purchased_or_leased.description: Condition of the vehicle when purchased or leased
options: Demo More than or Equal to 5,000 KM

date_of_purchase_or_lease.description: Date of purchase or lease in YYYY-MM-DD format

winter_tires.description: Whether the vehicle has winter tires

unrepaired_damage.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific vehicle. If there is no clear evidence indicating unrepaired damage, default to 'No'.

vehicle_rebuilt.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific vehicle. If there is no clear evidence indicating the vehicle has been rebuilt, default to 'No'.
vehicle_rebuilt.description: Whether the vehicle has been rebuilt

vehicle_photos_received.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific vehicle. If there is no clear evidence indicating vehicle photos have been received, default to 'No'.
vehicle_photos_received.description: Whether vehicle photos have been received

vehicle_modified_or_accessories.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific vehicle. If there is no clear evidence indicating the vehicle has been modified or has accessories, default to 'No'.
vehicle_modified_or_accessories.description: Whether the vehicle has been modified or has accessories

has_loan.extraction_logic: Extract from documents whether the vehicle has a loan, interest, finance, or lease. IMPORTANT: If this field is 'No', then all other fields in the 'interest' section (type_of_interest, client_selection_type, company_name) should NOT be included in the output JSON.
has_loan.description: Whether the vehicle has a loan or interest or finance or lease

type_of_interest.extraction_logic: Extract from documents. If the arrangement is a 'lease', select 'Lessor'. For all other cases (loan, finance, lien, etc.), select 'Lienholder'. Only include this field if has_loan is 'Yes'.
type_of_interest.description: Type of interest for interested party

client_selection_type.extraction_logic: If a matching company name is found in the corporate list (see company_name logic), select 'Select from corporate list'. If no matching company is found, select 'Create a new party'. Only include this field if has_loan is 'Yes'.
client_selection_type.description: Select an option for client selection

company_name.extraction_logic: Extract the company name from documents. Match it to the corporate list using fuzzy matching (e.g., 'BMO' matches 'Bank of Montreal', 'TD bank' or 'TD finance' matches 'TD Auto Finance', 'RBC' matches 'Royal Bank of Canada'). If a match is found, use the exact name from the list. If no match is found, set client_selection_type to 'Create a new party' and output the actual company name found in documents (even if it's not in the list). Only include this field if has_loan is 'Yes'.
options: Fédération des Caisses Desjardins du Québec

driver.description: Driver information - array of drivers. Extract ALL drivers found in documents. Each element represents one driver.
driver.extraction_logic: Extract ALL drivers found in the documents. Each driver should be a separate element in the array. If only one driver is found, the array should contain one element. Each driver element should contain all the driver fields for that specific driver. IMPORTANT for identity fields (last_name, first_name, gender, date_of_birth, marital_status, postal_code, full_address): include them ONLY for the second, third, fourth, ... driver (array index 1, 2, 3, ...). For the FIRST driver (index 0), OMIT these seven fields—they duplicate applicant_information and address at the root. For each additional driver, fill these with THAT driver's personal details and address from the policy; if the driver shares the same household as the applicant, you may use the same postal_code and full_address as root address.

last_name.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: this driver's last name. Omit on the first driver (index 0).
last_name.description: Additional driver's last name (omit on first driver in array)

first_name.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: this driver's first name. Omit on the first driver (index 0).
first_name.description: Additional driver's first name (omit on first driver in array)

gender.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: Female, Male, or X. Omit on the first driver (index 0).

date_of_birth.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format. Omit on the first driver (index 0).
date_of_birth.description: Additional driver's date of birth in YYYY-MM-DD format

marital_status.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: Divorced, Married, Single, or Widow. Omit on the first driver (index 0).

postal_code.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: this driver's postal code (Canadian X9X9X9). If same household as applicant, repeat root address.postal_code. Omit on the first driver (index 0).

full_address.extraction_logic: For drivers at index 1+ only: full address like '168 Trail Ridge Lane, Markham, ON'. If same household, repeat root address.full_address. Omit on the first driver (index 0).

student.description: Whether THIS specific driver is a student

driver_training_certificate.description: Whether THIS specific driver has a driver training certificate

licence_class.extraction_logic: Extract THIS specific driver's licence class from documents. IMPORTANT: Based on the selected licence_class, only include the corresponding date fields. For G-class licences: if licence_class is 'G1', only include g1_class_date_licensed; if licence_class is 'G2', include g1_class_date_licensed and g2_class_date_licensed (but NOT g_class_date_licensed); if licence_class is 'G', include all three: g1_class_date_licensed, g2_class_date_licensed, and g_class_date_licensed.

g_class_date_licensed.extraction_logic: Extract the G Class date licensed for THIS specific driver. ONLY include this field if licence_class is 'G'. Do NOT include if licence_class is 'G1' or 'G2'.
g_class_date_licensed.description: G Class - Date Licensed in YYYY-MM-DD format

g2_class_date_licensed.extraction_logic: Extract the G2 Class date licensed for THIS specific driver. Include this field if licence_class is 'G2' or 'G'. Do NOT include if licence_class is 'G1'.
g2_class_date_licensed.description: G2 Class - Date Licensed in YYYY-MM-DD format

g1_class_date_licensed.extraction_logic: Extract the G1 Class date licensed for THIS specific driver. Include this field if licence_class is 'G1', 'G2', or 'G'.
g1_class_date_licensed.description: G1 Class - Date Licensed in YYYY-MM-DD format

MVR_report_status.extraction_logic: Set MVR report status for THIS specific driver based on conviction result. If this driver has no conviction, select 'received-clean'. If this driver has any conviction detail/code (not 'No'), select 'received-with detail'.
MVR_report_status.description: MVR report status for THIS specific driver

MVR_request_date_time.description: MVR's Request Date/Time in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver

Consent_Date.extraction_logic: For THIS specific driver, compare two dates from documents and choose the earlier one: (1) MVR header date from pattern like '*** MOTOR VEHICLE RECORD - YYYY/MM/DD ***'; (2) AutoPlus 'Report Date'. Output the earlier date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
Consent_Date.description: Consent date in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver (earlier of MVR date and AutoPlus Report Date)

insurance_history_report_status.extraction_logic: Extract from dash/autoplus documents for THIS specific driver. Check if the insurance history report is detected or has specific content. If dash/autoplus documents are missing (not provided) for this driver, treat it as no insurance history record and select 'Not Found'. Select 'Not Found' if not detected in dash/autoplus, 'Not Ordered' if not ordered, 'Received' if received, or 'Ordered - Pending' if ordered but pending.
insurance_history_report_status.description: Insurance history report status for THIS specific driver

insurance_history_report_request_date.extraction_logic: Extract the insurance history report request date for THIS specific driver. If not found in documents, use the same value as 'MVR_request_date_time' (MVR's Request Date/Time).
insurance_history_report_request_date.description: Insurance History Report Request Date in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver

convictions.extraction_logic: Extract from MVR documents for THIS specific driver. If convictions are found, analyze the conviction description in the MVR document and match it to the most appropriate conviction code from the options list. Each code represents a specific violation type (e.g., 'ALCAF' = 'Accompanying Driver - Fail or Refuse to Provide Breath Sample', 'CDR' = 'Careless Driving', 'SP50M' = 'Speeding - Less than 50 KM over Speed Limit'). Select the code that best matches the conviction description found in the MVR. If no convictions are found in the MVR, select 'No'.
convictions.description: THIS specific driver's conviction type. Select 'No' if no convictions found.

licence_suspensions.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific driver. Default to 'No' unless there is clear evidence in the documents indicating that the driver has licence suspensions. Only select 'Yes' if there is explicit information about licence suspensions.
licence_suspensions.description: Whether THIS specific driver has licence suspensions

lapse_in_insurance.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific driver. If 'Yes', then the 'lapse_in_insurance_description' field must also be included in the output.
lapse_in_insurance.description: Whether there is a lapse in insurance for THIS specific driver

lapse_in_insurance_description.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific driver. This field is only required if 'lapse_in_insurance' is 'Yes'. Select the most appropriate description for the insurance lapse from the options.
lapse_in_insurance_description.description: Description of the insurance lapse for THIS specific driver

lapse_start.extraction_logic: Only extract when 'lapse_in_insurance' is 'Yes'. Map from the Lapses section Date field for THIS specific driver.
lapse_start.description: Lapse start date in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver

lapse_end.extraction_logic: Only extract when 'lapse_in_insurance' is 'Yes'. Map from the Lapses section Re-Instate Date field for THIS specific driver.
lapse_end.description: Lapse end date in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver

insured_without_interruption_since.description: Insured Without Interruption Since in YYYY-MM-DD format for THIS specific driver

risk_type.extraction_logic: Extract from documents for THIS specific driver. Default to 'Private Passenger Vehicle' unless there is clear evidence indicating a different vehicle type (All terrain Vehicle, Antique, Classic, Motorcycle, Motorhome, Snowmobile, or Trailer). Only select a different option if there is explicit information about the vehicle type.
risk_type.description: Risk type or vehicle type for THIS specific driver

previous_insurer.extraction_logic: Extract the previous insurer name from documents for THIS specific driver. Match it to the list using fuzzy matching - the match does not need to be exact, but should identify the same company (e.g., 'BMO' matches 'Bank of Montreal', 'TD bank' or 'TD finance' matches 'TD Meloche Monnex (Security National)' or 'TD General Insurance Company', 'RBC' matches 'Royal Bank of Canada'). If a match is found, use the exact name from the list. If no match is found and there is clearly no prior insurer, select 'No Prior Insurer'.
previous_insurer.description: Previous insurer name for THIS specific driver
options: Amherst Island Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Bay of Quinte Agricultural Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Bertie and Clinton Mutual Insurance Company
Caradoc Delaware Mutual Fire Insurance Company
East Williams Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Glengarry Farmers' Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Grey and Bruce Mutual Insurance Company
Hamilton Township Farmers Mutual Fire Insurance Company
ICBC - Insurance Corporation of British Columbia
Kent and Essex Mutual Insurance Company
Lennox and Addington Mutual Fire Insurance Company
North Kent Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Other Canada or U.S. Insurer not on the list
Peel and Maryborough Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Portage la Praire Mutual Insurance Company
State Farm Mutual Automobile Insurance Company
TD Home and Auto Insurance Company
TD Home and Auto Insurance Company (FA)
Townsend Farmers' Mutual Fire Insurance Company
Usborne and Hibbert Mutual Fire Insurance Company

expiry_date.extraction_logic: Extract the expiry date from the previous insurance information in documents for THIS specific driver. This is the end date of the previous insurance policy in YYYY-MM-DD format.
expiry_date.description: Expiry date in YYYY-MM-DD format (from previous insurance) for THIS specific driver

previous_insurer_policy_number.extraction_logic: Extract the policy number from the previous insurance information in documents for THIS specific driver.
previous_insurer_policy_number.description: Previous insurer policy number (from previous insurance) for THIS specific driver

number_of_years_with_previous_insurer.extraction_logic: Calculate the number of years for THIS specific driver by subtracting the previous insurance effective date from the expiry_date. Use rounding up (ceiling) - if it's more than 3 years even by one day, it counts as 4 years. The result must be an integer. For example: if effective date is 2020-01-01 and expiry date is 2023-01-02, that's 3 years and 1 day, which rounds up to 4 years.
number_of_years_with_previous_insurer.description: Number of years with previous insurer (calculated, rounded up to integer) for THIS specific driver

assignment.description: Assignment information. Includes per-vehicle driver usage (vehicle_1, vehicle_2, ...) aligned with quote 'Vehicle N of M' blocks and their Drivers lists, plus vehicle use and mileage fields.

vehicle_1.extraction_logic: For the FIRST vehicle on the quote (e.g. 'Vehicle 1 of M'), output an object whose keys are driver_1, driver_2, driver_3, ... in the SAME order as drivers listed under THAT vehicle's Drivers section. Each driver_k MUST be an object with: (1) name: string — full name as printed (e.g. 'Ai Wang'); strip trailing role tags like '(Prn)' / '(Occ)' from the name value if they appear only as labels. (2) percentage_of_use: integer 0–100. DEFAULT (covers most quotes): the quote usually only indicates WHO is Principal vs Occasional via labels like (Prn) and (Occ), NOT numeric percentages — in that case you MUST fill percentage_of_use using ONLY these FIXED rules by driver count N on THIS vehicle: N=1 → 100 for the sole driver. N=2 → Prn 60, Occ 40. N=3 → Prn 60, each Occ 20. N=4 → Prn 70, each Occ 10. N=5 → Prn 60, each Occ 10. Match each value to the correct person by (Prn)/(Occ), not by list position alone. EXCEPTION (rare): if the quote explicitly prints a numeric 'Percentage of Use (%)' (or equivalent) per driver, use those printed values instead of the fixed rules. If N>5: use printed percentages when present; otherwise Prn 60 and split remaining 40 equally among Occ drivers (integers summing to 100). Omit vehicle_1 entirely only if the quote provides no per-vehicle driver listing for vehicle 1.
vehicle_1.description: Vehicle 1 driver usage: nested driver_1, driver_2, ... each with name and percentage_of_use (0-100); default % grid by Prn/Occ count per rules in extraction_logic.

vehicle_2.extraction_logic: Same structure and rules as vehicle_1, but for the SECOND vehicle on the quote ('Vehicle 2 of M'). Omit if there is no second vehicle.
vehicle_2.description: Vehicle 2 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_3.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the third vehicle. Omit if fewer than three vehicles.
vehicle_3.description: Vehicle 3 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_4.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the fourth vehicle. Omit if fewer than four vehicles.
vehicle_4.description: Vehicle 4 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_5.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the fifth vehicle. Omit if fewer than five vehicles.
vehicle_5.description: Vehicle 5 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_6.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the sixth vehicle. Omit if fewer than six vehicles.
vehicle_6.description: Vehicle 6 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_7.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the seventh vehicle. Omit if fewer than seven vehicles.
vehicle_7.description: Vehicle 7 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

vehicle_8.extraction_logic: Same as vehicle_1 for the eighth vehicle. Omit if fewer than eight vehicles.
vehicle_8.description: Vehicle 8 driver usage (same shape as vehicle_1).

type_of_use.extraction_logic: Extract from documents. If uncertain or no relevant information is found, default to 'Pleasure'. Only select other options (Business, Farmer Personal Use, Vocational) if there is clear evidence in the documents.
type_of_use.description: Type of use for the vehicle

km_toward_work.extraction_logic: Extract kilometers driven toward work. Priority: first check the quote document, if not found check other documents.

annual_km.extraction_logic: Extract annual kilometers driven. Priority: first check the quote document, if not found check other documents.

annual_business_km.extraction_logic: Extract annual business kilometers driven. IMPORTANT: If type_of_use is 'Pleasure', annual_business_km must always be 0. For other type_of_use values, extract from documents. Priority: first check the quote document, if not found check other documents. If the value cannot be found in any document, output 0. Do not make up or guess values.

automobile_rented_or_leased_to_others.extraction_logic: If there is no clear evidence that the automobile is rented or leased to others, must select 'No'. Only select 'Yes' if there is explicit evidence in the documents.
automobile_rented_or_leased_to_others.description: Whether the automobile is rented or leased to others

automobile_used_to_carry_passengers_for_.extraction_logic: If there is no clear evidence that the automobile is used to carry passengers for compensation or hire, must select 'No'. Only select 'Yes' if there is explicit evidence in the documents.
automobile_used_to_carry_passengers_for_.description: Whether the automobile is used to carry passengers for compensation or hire

automobile_carry_explosives_or_radioacti.extraction_logic: If there is no clear evidence that the automobile carries explosives or radioactive materials, must select 'No'. Only select 'Yes' if there is explicit evidence in the documents.
automobile_carry_explosives_or_radioacti.description: Whether the automobile carries explosives or radioactive materials

has_claim.extraction_logic: If 'No' is selected, all other fields in the 'claim' section (date_of_loss, at_fault, cause_of_loss, total_amount_paid, coverage, assigned_vehicle) should not be present in the output. Only extract other claim fields if 'Yes' is selected. If 'Yes', extract ALL claims found in the documents - there can be multiple claims, and each claim should have corresponding entries in all claim fields (arrays should have the same length, with index i representing claim i).

date_of_loss.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Extract ALL dates of loss for ALL claims found in the documents. Each date should be in YYYY-MM-DD format. Output as an array, where each element corresponds to one claim. If there are multiple claims, this array should have multiple elements.
date_of_loss.description: Date of loss in YYYY-MM-DD format (array - one element per claim)

at_fault.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Extract whether each claim was at fault. Output as an array, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found.
at_fault.description: Whether at fault (array - one element per claim)

cause_of_loss.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Select the most appropriate cause of loss from the options for EACH claim found. Output as an array, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found.
cause_of_loss.description: Cause of loss (array - one element per claim)
options: Enter or Exit Driveway or Parking Lot
INS Wrong Way One Way Street
TP Wrong Way One Way Street

total_amount_paid.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Extract the total amount paid for EACH claim found. Output as an array of numbers, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found.
total_amount_paid.description: Total amount paid in currency (array - one element per claim)

coverage.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Select the coverage type for EACH claim found. Output as an array, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found.
coverage.description: Coverage type (array - one element per claim)

assigned_vehicle.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Extract the vehicle information for EACH claim found (e.g., vehicle description, VIN, or vehicle identifier). Output as an array of strings, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found.
assigned_vehicle.description: The vehicle that this claim is assigned to (array - one element per claim)

assigned_driver.extraction_logic: Only extract if has_claim is 'Yes'. Extract the driver information for EACH claim found (e.g., driver full name or clear driver identifier). Output as an array of strings, where each element corresponds to one claim. The array length should match the number of claims found and should align by index with date_of_loss/at_fault/cause_of_loss/total_amount_paid/coverage/assigned_vehicle.
assigned_driver.description: The driver that this claim is assigned to (array - one element per claim)

limit.extraction_logic: Extract from the quote document. Look for the value following 'Bodily Injury'. Convert abbreviations: 1m = 1000000, 2m = 2000000, etc. Select the matching value from the dropdown options.
limit.description: Limit amount in currency (typically found after 'Bodily Injury' in quote document)

additional_coverages.extraction_logic: Extract coverages and their corresponding limit values from the quote document that are NOT already included in the claim section's coverage field. For each coverage found, output the coverage name and its limit value (e.g., 'Coverage Name: Value'). Exclude coverages that are already recorded in claim.coverage. IMPORTANT: Exclude 'Property Damage' (it is already represented by the 'limit' field and has the same value). Also exclude 'All Perils' (it is already displayed in the 'all_perils_or_collision_or_upset' field).
additional_coverages.description: Array of coverages and their limit values that are not included in the claim section. Excludes Property Damage (same as limit) and All Perils (already in all_perils_or_collision_or_upset field).

accident_benefits_standard_benefits.extraction_logic: Scan the quote document to identify which options are selected. Use fuzzy matching - if the document mentions something that is semantically the same as an option (even if the wording is slightly different), select that option. When a coverage option is found, also check if there is a numerical value following it (e.g., '50K' = 50000, '1M' = 1000000). If a value is found, output the option name followed by the value in the format 'Option Name: Value' (e.g., 'Increased AB - Income Replacement: 50000'). Convert abbreviations: K = thousands (50K = 50000), M = millions (1M = 1000000).
accident_benefits_standard_benefits.description: Accident Benefits (Standard Benefits) optional coverage - can select multiple options. If a coverage has an associated limit value in the quote, include it in the format 'Option Name: Value'. Use fuzzy matching to identify similar coverage names.
options: Increased AB - Caregiver, Housekeeping & Home Maintenance
Increased AB - Medical, Rehabilitation and Attendant Care

dcpd_deductible.description: Direct Compensation - Property Damage (DCPD) deductible amount

all_perils_or_collision_or_upset.description: ALL Perils OR Collision or Upset coverage type

all_perils_or_collision_or_upset_deducti.description: Deductible amount for ALL Perils OR Collision or Upset coverage

specified_perils_or_comprehensive.description: Specified Perils OR Comprehensive coverage type

specified_perils_or_comprehensive_deduct.description: Deductible amount for Specified Perils OR Comprehensive coverage

section_optional_coverages.extraction_logic: Scan the quote document to identify which options are selected. Use fuzzy matching - if the document mentions something that is semantically the same as an option (even if the wording is slightly different), select that option. When a coverage option is found, also check if there is a numerical value following it (e.g., '#27 Liab to Unowned Veh. 50K' should record 'Liab to Unowned Veh.: 50000'). If a value is found, output the option name followed by the value in the format 'Option Name: Value' (e.g., 'OPCF 20 - Coverage for Transportation Replacement: 50000'). Convert abbreviations: K = thousands (50K = 50000), M = millions (1M = 1000000).
section_optional_coverages.description: Section optional coverages - can select multiple options. If a coverage has an associated limit value in the quote, include it in the format 'Option Name: Value'. Use fuzzy matching to identify similar coverage names.
options: OPCF 6A - Permission to Carry Paying Passengers
OPCF 48 - Added Coverage to Offset Tort Deductibles
OPCF 2 - Drive, Rent or Lease other Automobiles
OPCF 28 - Reducing Coverage for Named Persons

automobile_insurance_cancelled_or_refuse.extraction_logic: If there is no clear evidence that automobile insurance was cancelled or refused in the last 3 years, must select 'No'. Only select 'Yes' if there is explicit evidence in the documents.
automobile_insurance_cancelled_or_refuse.description: Automobile Insurance Cancelled or Refused in Last 3 Years

ubi_consent.extraction_logic: Check the quote document for 'W0048 - MY DRIVE-ACTIVATION (NEW BUS ONLY) INCLUDED' or any MY DRIVE related content. Also check the application document for mentions of MY DRIVE. If MY DRIVE is found in either the quote or application, select 'Yes'. Otherwise, select 'No'.

named_insureds_have_additional_policies_.extraction_logic: Always select 'No' for this field, regardless of document content.
named_insureds_have_additional_policies_.description: Do the Named Insureds have additional policies with Intact Financial Corporation
